user_message: Give me the manager view on this Northwind Traders support ticket.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: migration architecture Discussion - Northwind Traders
Date: 2024-03-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on migration architecture from our last session.

[00:06:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Let me add some context -- we've been working on vendor dependencies and found that the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk.

[00:10:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): Let me add some context -- we've been working on data residency and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:13:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is stakeholder alignment.

[00:15:00] Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is security compliance.

[00:18:00] Sandra Taylor (Northwind Traders): The timeline for cost optimization is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

handoffs.condition: ECIF workscope or partner engagement warranted

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF49-D trap (Confuses workshop discussion with workscope commitment); produces complete Workshop recap, ECIF workscope draft (if warranted), partner plan, follow-up items with owners, mile
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF49-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF49-D failure mode (Confuses workshop discussion with workscope commitment)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct migration architecture decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF49-D -- Confuses workshop discussion with workscope commitment
